source: Generalizing from a Few Examples- A Survey on Few-Shot Learning.pdf

concepts.finding: FSL methods categorized into: data (augment limited supervised experience), model (constrain hypothesis space via parameter sharing, embedding learning), and algorithm (meta-learning, optimization-based).
concepts.implication: FSL taxonomy provides a framework for handling new query types with minimal labeled examples, critical for rapidly adapting search to emerging topics.
concepts.open_question: Which FSL approach is most data-efficient for adapting query intent classifiers to new categories with <10 labeled examples?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A meta-learning (MAML) approach for few-shot query intent classification will achieve 85%+ accuracy with only 5 examples per new intent class.
